steps.title: Model the rental cost
steps.content:
Renting for $x$ days costs:
  • $270 for the first day (flat)
  • $135 for each additional day
Additional days = $(x - 1)$, so:
$$y = 270 + 135(x - 1)$$

steps.title: Expand to match the answer choice format
steps.content:
$$y = 270 + 135x - 135 = 135x + 135$$
Verify with $x = 1$: $y = 135(1) + 135 = 270$. Correct — only the first-day rate. Verify with $x = 2$: $y = 135(2) + 135 = 405 = 270 + 135$. Correct.

steps.title: Evaluate all choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$y = 270x - 135$': At $x=1$, gives $270 - 135 = 135$. But renting for 1 day should cost $270. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = 270x + 135$': At $x=1$, gives $270 + 135 = 405$. Way too high for a single day. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 135x + 270$': At $x=1$, gives $135 + 270 = 405$. Too high. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 135x + 135$': At $x=1$, gives $135 + 135 = 270$. Correct. At $x=2$, gives $405 = 270 + 135$. Correct. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.

explanation:
• Choice A — '$y = 270x - 135$': At $x = 1$ gives $135$, not the required $270. ✗
  • Choice B — '$y = 270x + 135$': At $x = 1$ gives $405$ — far too high; also grows at $270 per day rather than $135. ✗
  • Choice C — '$y = 135x + 270$': At $x = 1$ gives $405$; the intercept should be $135$, not $270. ✗
  • Choice D — '$y = 135x + 135$': Derived from $270 + 135(x-1) = 135x + 135$; gives $270$ at $x=1$ and $405$ at $x=2$. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
